# Let's load the two JSON files from the given paths.
import json, os, sys, re
qpath = '/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json'
hpath = '/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json'
print(os.path.exists(qpath), os.path.exists(hpath))
with open(qpath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    qdata = json.load(f)
len_q = len(qdata)
print("quranic verses entries:", len_q)
# Peek first entry
qdata[:2]

# search for candidates in qdata by normalized matching methods
import unicodedata, math
def normalize_ar(text):
    # lowercase (not relevant for Arabic), remove tatweel, punctuation, diacritics, unify hamza forms
    text = str(text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640', '')
    # normalize different alef forms to bare alef
    text = re.sub('[إأآا]', 'ا', text)
    # normalise hamza on waw/ya? maybe convert ؤ ئ to و ي? Better unify hamzas to basic hamza 'ء'
    # but instruction says unify hamza; we'll map ؤ ئ to ا? Hmm better to replace with ء 
    text = re.sub('[ؤئإأآ]', 'ء', text)
    # remove diacritics (tashkeel)
    arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]""")
    text = re.sub(arabic_diacritics, '', text)
    # remove punctuation and non letters/digits
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\u0621-\u064A\s]', ' ', text)
    # replace underscores
    text = text.replace('_',' ')
    # collapse spaces and lowercase
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

query_full = "الرجال قوامون على النساء بما فضل الله بعضهم على بعض وبما أنفقوا من أموالهم"
qnorm = normalize_ar(query_full)
qnorm

# find the exact DB entry for surah 4 ayah 34
candidate_entry = None
for entry in qdata:
    if entry['surah_id']==4 and entry['ayah_id']==34:
        candidate_entry = entry
        break
candidate_entry

# define functions for metrics again
from difflib import SequenceMatcher
def normalized_lcs_ratio(a,b):
    a_norm = normalize_ar(a)
    b_norm = normalize_ar(b)
    m = SequenceMatcher(None,a_norm,b_norm).find_longest_match(0,len(a_norm),0,len(b_norm))
    return m.size / max(1, len(a_norm)), m.size, a_norm, b_norm

# For token_f1, compute between normalized forms a->candidate? But we want similarity between candidate and FULL substring.
# We'll compute f1 as earlier token_f1(normalized_query, normalized_candidate)
norm_candidate = normalize_ar(candidate_entry['ayah_text'])

full = query_full
full_norm = normalize_ar(full)
lcs_ratio, lcs_size, a_norm, b_norm = normalized_lcs_ratio(full, candidate_entry['ayah_text'])
f1_full = token_f1(full_norm, norm_candidate)
seq_full = SequenceMatcher(None, full_norm, norm_candidate).ratio()

snippet = "الرجال قوا...من أموالهم"
snip_processed = snippet.replace('...',' ')
snip_norm = normalize_ar(snip_processed)

lcs_ratio_snip, lcs_size_snip, a_norm_snip, b_norm_snip = normalized_lcs_ratio(snip_processed, candidate_entry['ayah_text'])
f1_snip = token_f1(snip_norm, norm_candidate)
seq_snip = SequenceMatcher(None, snip_norm, norm_candidate).ratio()

full_norm, norm_candidate, {
    'lcs_ratio_full': lcs_ratio,
    'lcs_size_full': lcs_size,
    'f1_full': f1_full,
    'seq_full': seq_full,
    'snip_norm': snip_norm,
    'lcs_ratio_snip': lcs_ratio_snip,
    'lcs_size_snip': lcs_size_snip,
    'f1_snip': f1_snip,
    'seq_snip': seq_snip
}

with open(hpath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    hdata = json.load(f)
len_h = len(hdata)
len_h

# compute similarity (sequence ratio) for hadith Matn/hadithTxt fields and get top matches
from heapq import nlargest
top = []
for entry in hdata:
    best_ratio = 0.0
    best_field = None
    for field in ('Matn','hadithTxt'):
        txt = entry.get(field)
        if not txt:
            continue
        tnorm = normalize_ar(txt)
        ratio = SequenceMatcher(None, qnorm, tnorm).ratio()
        if ratio > best_ratio:
            best_ratio = ratio
            best_field = field
    if best_ratio > 0.2: # threshold to reduce results
        top.append((best_ratio, best_field, entry))
top_sorted = sorted(top, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
top_sorted[:10]

content.text: {"correction":"الرِّجَالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ بِمَا فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ وَبِمَا أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ أَمْوَالِهِمْ ۚ فَالصَّالِحَاتُ قَانِتَاتٌ حَافِظَاتٌ لِلْغَيْبِ بِمَا حَفِظَ اللَّهُ ۚ وَاللَّاتِي تَخَافُونَ نُشُوزَهُنَّ فَعِظُوهُنَّ وَاهْجُرُوهُنَّ فِي الْمَضَاجِعِ وَاضْرِبُوهُنَّ ۖ فَإِنْ أَطَعْنَكُمْ فَلَا تَبْغُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ سَبِيلًا ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيًّا كَبِيرًا"}
